solution.p: We will factor 5r^{2}-19r+18 where a=5, b=-19, and c=18.
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (5) \cdot (18) = 90.

The number 90 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(90)\amp -1(-90)\amp 2(45)\amp -2(-45)
ewline3(30)\amp -3(-30)\amp 5(18)\amp -5(-18)
ewline6(15)\amp -6(-15)\amp 9(10)\amp -9(-10)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -19, and this sum happens with (-10)+(-9)=-19 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]5r^{2}-19r+18\amp =5r^{2}-10r-9r+18
ewline \amp =5r\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)-9\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)
ewline \amp =\left(5r-9ight)\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 5r^{2}-19r+18 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 5, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(5)\amp \end{array}
The third term, 18, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(18)\amp -1(-18)\amp 2(9)\amp -2(-9)\newline3(6)\amp -3(-6)\amp \end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 5r^{2}-19r+18 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]5r^{2}-19r+18\amp =\left(5r-9\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-2\right)\end{aligned}
